notes: Pass rate: with_skill 100% vs baseline 28.6% (+71pp). The skill is doing real work, not redundant scaffolding.

Both configurations reach the right CONCLUSION roughly equally well — baseline correctly recommends deletion in eval-1 and eval-3 and correctly identifies orchestration as the bug location in eval-2. The skill differentiates by DISCIPLINE: named heuristics (deletion test), precise vocabulary (seam/adapter/leverage/locality), explicit anti-pattern naming, and the workflow ending ('Which of these would you like to explore?').

Assertion 'Does NOT propose new interface signatures' (eval-1) may be non-discriminating in this prompt set since none of the prompts ask for interface design. To stress-test the 'discuss before designing' rule, future evals should include a prompt that tempts the model to jump to designing.
Token overhead from skill: +2,643 avg per run (~17%) — primarily from reading the 125-line SKILL.md.
Time overhead from skill: +10.4s avg per run (~31%) — substantial but acceptable for the +71pp pass rate gain.
Single-run-per-configuration limits variance analysis. If the skill ships, consider 3-run reruns for assertions that hover near the pass/fail boundary.
